# provenance: the HONEST (non-masked) GraphQL rate-limit error text --
# gh's rate-limit errors DO contain "rate limit" wording when they aren't
# masked as something else (contrast masked-unknown-owner-type.txt in this
# same corpus). Requested verbatim shape per the #91 task brief: GraphQL:
# API rate limit already exceeded for user ID <n>. This exact byte string
# was not found committed verbatim anywhere in this repo's history (grepped
# git log -p and .claude/feedbacks/feed.yaml) -- it is RECONSTRUCTED from
# GitHub's documented GraphQL rate-limit error format (the "user ID <n>"
# variant applies to OAuth/PAT-authenticated callers, as this session's own
# `gh auth status` shows: account Zugruul, a personal token, not a GitHub
# App installation -- the "installation ID <n>" variant the OLD inline
# fixture strings used applies only to GitHub App installation tokens,
# which this project's gh calls never use).
# not live-captured: reproducing it for real requires actually exhausting
# graphql quota, and this session's real quota was already down to 41
# remaining (see rate-limit-endpoint-sample.json in this corpus) before any
# deliberate exhaustion -- burning it further would have risked starving
# concurrent lanes/agents sharing this account's real GraphQL budget, so
# this task did not perform the optional deliberate-exhaustion recipe.
# source: task #91 brief (from issues #74/#77 history); GitHub's documented
# GraphQL RATE_LIMITED error message format; `gh auth status` (this session).

GraphQL: API rate limit already exceeded for user ID 4837291. (createProjectV2Item)
